Integration test for the core generation function:

```typescript
import { describe, expect, test } from 'vitest'
import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'
import { generateRestClient } from '../generateRestClient'

const stubModels: DMMF.Model[] = [
  {
    name: 'User',
    dbName: null,
    schema: null,
    fields: [
      { kind: 'scalar', name: 'id', type: 'Int', isRequired: true, isList: false, isUnique: false, isId: true, isReadOnly: false, hasDefaultValue: true },
      { kind: 'scalar', name: 'email', type: 'String', isRequired: true, isList: false, isUnique: true, isId: false, isReadOnly: false, hasDefaultValue: false },
      { kind: 'scalar', name: 'name', type: 'String', isRequired: false, isList: false, isUnique: false, isId: false, isReadOnly: false, hasDefaultValue: false },
      { kind: 'object', name: 'posts', type: 'Post', isRequired: false, isList: true, isUnique: false, isId: false, isReadOnly: false, hasDefaultValue: false, relationName: 'UserPosts', relationFromFields: [], relationToFields: [] },
      { kind: 'enum', name: 'role', type: 'Role', isRequired: true, isList: false, isUnique: false, isId: false, isReadOnly: false, hasDefaultValue: true },
    ],
    uniqueFields: [],
    uniqueIndexes: [],
    primaryKey: null,
  },
]

const stubEnums: DMMF.DatamodelEnum[] = [
  {
    name: 'Role',
    values: [
      { name: 'ADMIN', dbName: null },
      { name: 'USER', dbName: null },
    ],
  },
]

describe('generateRestClient', () => {
  test('generates valid TypeScript with interfaces, enums, and client class', () => {
    const output = generateRestClient(stubModels, stubEnums)

    // Contains preamble
    expect(output).toContain('// This file was generated by prisma-generator-rest-client')

    // Contains enum
    expect(output).toContain('export enum Role')
    expect(output).toContain('ADMIN = "ADMIN"')

    // Contains model interface with scalar + enum fields, no relation fields
    expect(output).toContain('export interface User')
    expect(output).toContain('id: number')
    expect(output).toContain('email: string')
    expect(output).toContain('name?: string')
    expect(output).toContain('role: Role')
    expect(output).not.toContain('posts')

    // Contains create input (excludes fields with defaults)
    expect(output).toContain('export interface UserCreateInput')

    // Contains update input (all optional, excludes id)
    expect(output).toContain('export interface UserUpdateInput')

    // Contains client class with CRUD methods
    expect(output).toContain('export class RestClient')
    expect(output).toContain('userFindMany')
    expect(output).toContain('userFindUnique')
    expect(output).toContain('userCreate')
    expect(output).toContain('userUpdate')
    expect(output).toContain('userDelete')

    // Contains config type
    expect(output).toContain('export interface RestClientConfig')
    expect(output).toContain('baseUrl: string')
  })

  test('handles models with no enum fields', () => {
    const output = generateRestClient(stubModels, [])
    expect(output).toContain('export interface User')
    expect(output).toContain('export class RestClient')
  })
})

### Example Generated Output

For a Prisma schema containing:

```prisma
enum Role {
  ADMIN
  USER
}

model User {
  id    Int     @id @default(autoincrement())
  email String  @unique
  name  String?
  role  Role    @default(USER)
  posts Post[]
}

model Post {
  id        Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  title     String
  content   String?
  published Boolean  @default(false)
  authorId  Int
  author    User     @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id])
}
```

The generated `rest-client.ts` would be:

```typescript
// This file was generated by prisma-generator-rest-client. Do not edit manually.
// Re-run `prisma generate` to regenerate.

export interface RestClientConfig {
  baseUrl: string;
  headers?: Record<string, string>;
}

export enum Role {
  ADMIN = "ADMIN",
  USER = "USER",
}

export interface User {
  id: number;
  email: string;
  name?: string;
  role: Role;
}

export interface Post {
  id: number;
  title: string;
  content?: string;
  published: boolean;
  authorId: number;
}

export interface UserCreateInput {
  email: string;
  name?: string;
}

export interface UserUpdateInput {
  email?: string;
  name?: string;
  role?: Role;
}

export interface PostCreateInput {
  title: string;
  content?: string;
  authorId: number;
}

export interface PostUpdateInput {
  title?: string;
  content?: string;
  published?: boolean;
  authorId?: number;
}

export class RestClient {
  private config: RestClientConfig;

  constructor(config: RestClientConfig) {
    this.config = config;
  }

  private async request<T>(path: string, options?: RequestInit): Promise<T> {
    const url = `${this.config.baseUrl}${path}`;
    const response = await fetch(url, {
      ...options,
      headers: {
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
        ...this.config.headers,
        ...options?.headers,
      },
    });
    if (!response.ok) {
      throw new Error(`REST client error: ${response.status} ${response.statusText}`);
    }
    return response.json() as Promise<T>;
  }

  // --- User ---

  async userFindMany(): Promise<User[]> {
    return this.request<User[]>("/users");
  }

  async userFindUnique(id: number): Promise<User> {
    return this.request<User>(`/users/${id}`);
  }

  async userCreate(data: UserCreateInput): Promise<User> {
    return this.request<User>("/users", {
      method: "POST",
      body: JSON.stringify(data),
    });
  }

  async userUpdate(id: number, data: UserUpdateInput): Promise<User> {
    return this.request<User>(`/users/${id}`, {
      method: "PATCH",
      body: JSON.stringify(data),
    });
  }

  async userDelete(id: number): Promise<User> {
    return this.request<User>(`/users/${id}`, {
      method: "DELETE",
    });
  }

  // --- Post ---

  async postFindMany(): Promise<Post[]> {
    return this.request<Post[]>("/posts");
  }

  async postFindUnique(id: number): Promise<Post> {
    return this.request<Post>(`/posts/${id}`);
  }

  async postCreate(data: PostCreateInput): Promise<Post> {
    return this.request<Post>("/posts", {
      method: "POST",
      body: JSON.stringify(data),
    });
  }

  async postUpdate(id: number, data: PostUpdateInput): Promise<Post> {
    return this.request<Post>(`/posts/${id}`, {
      method: "PATCH",
      body: JSON.stringify(data),
    });
  }

  async postDelete(id: number): Promise<Post> {
    return this.request<Post>(`/posts/${id}`, {
      method: "DELETE",
    });
  }
}
```

---

### Implementation Sequencing

**Phase 1: Scaffolding (no logic)**
1. Create `package.json` with dependencies and bin entry
2. Create `tsconfig.json` and `tsconfig.build.json`
3. Create `helpers/build.ts`

**Phase 2: Core generation logic**
4. Create `src/scalarMapping.ts` (no dependencies, testable immediately)
5. Create `src/template.ts` (no dependencies, testable immediately)
6. Create `src/generateRestClient.ts` (depends on scalarMapping + template)
7. Create `src/onManifest.ts` (standalone)
8. Create `src/onGenerate.ts` (depends on generateRestClient)

**Phase 3: Entry points**
9. Create `src/bin.ts` (depends on onManifest + onGenerate)
10. Create `src/index.ts` (re-exports)

**Phase 4: Tests**
11. Create `src/__tests__/scalarMapping.test.ts`
12. Create `src/__tests__/template.test.ts`
13. Create `src/__tests__/generateRestClient.test.ts`
14. Create `src/__tests__/onGenerate.test.ts`

**Phase 5: Build and verify**
15. Run `pnpm install` to link the new workspace package
16. Run `pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client build`
17. Verify `dist/bin.js` starts with `#!/usr/bin/env node`
18. Run `pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client test`

---

### Potential Challenges and Mitigations

1. **Shebang in bundled output**: The esbuild `banner` option with `{ js: '#!/usr/bin/env node' }` is the correct approach. After build, verify the file starts with the shebang and is executable (may need a post-build `chmod +x dist/bin.js` step, though npm/pnpm should handle this when the `bin` field is declared).

2. **ReadonlyDeep types from DMMF**: The DMMF types use `ReadonlyDeep<>`, meaning all properties are `readonly`. The generation code only reads from these objects, never mutates them, so this is not an issue. But when constructing test fixtures, you may need `as unknown as DMMF.Model[]` casts or satisfy the full readonly shape.

3. **Composite primary keys**: Some models use `@@id([fieldA, fieldB])` instead of `@id` on a single field. In this case, `model.primaryKey` is set and no individual field has `isId: true`. The plan handles this with a fallback: if no `isId` field is found, fall back to `'id'` as the param name and `'string'` as the type. A future enhancement could support composite keys with a separate `findUnique` signature.

4. **The `pluralize` function is naive**: For production quality, one could use the `pluralize` npm package (already used in `client-generator-ts`). The plan keeps it simple with a local helper for now, but this is an obvious improvement point.

5. **Generator not found at runtime**: When the user writes `provider = "prisma-generator-rest-client"`, the Prisma CLI resolves this name via `node_modules/.bin/`. The package must be installed in the user's project. The `bin` field in `package.json` ensures pnpm/npm creates the symlink.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775388216656-2srcvx/packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts` -- the JSON-RPC handler our bin.ts must call; defines the `Handler` interface contract
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775388216656-2srcvx/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts` -- the DMMF type definitions (Model, Field, DatamodelEnum, FieldKind) that the generator must consume
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775388216656-2srcvx/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts` -- the GeneratorOptions, GeneratorConfig, and GeneratorManifest types that define onGenerate/onManifest signatures
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775388216656-2srcvx/helpers/compile/configs.ts` -- the `createAdapterConfig` and `adapterConfig` used by our `helpers/build.ts`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775388216656-2srcvx/helpers/compile/build.ts` -- the esbuild build pipeline that our `helpers/build.ts` invokes

tool_use_result.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for a new Prisma generator package at `packages/generator-rest-client/` that generates a typed REST API client from a Prisma schema.

## Context

This is a Prisma monorepo. I've thoroughly explored how existing generators work. Here's what I found:

### Generator Protocol
- Generators use `generatorHandler` from `@prisma/generator-helper` which communicates via JSON-RPC over stdio
- The handler takes `{ onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions), onManifest?(config: GeneratorConfig) }`
- `onManifest` returns `GeneratorManifest` with `prettyName`, `defaultOutput`, etc.
- `onGenerate` receives `GeneratorOptions` containing `dmmf: DMMF.Document`, `generator: GeneratorConfig` (with `output` path), `schemaPath`, etc.
- `DMMF.Document.datamodel.models` is an array of `Model` objects with `name`, `fields[]` etc.
- Each `Field` has: `kind` ('scalar'|'object'|'enum'|'unsupported'), `name`, `type` (e.g. "String","Int","DateTime","$ModelName"), `isRequired`, `isList`, `isId`, `isUnique`, `hasDefaultValue`, `relationFromFields?`, `relationToFields?`, `relationName?`

### Package Structure Convention
Every generator package has:
```
packages/<name>/
├── package.json         # name: @prisma/<name>, deps on workspace:* packages
├── tsconfig.json        # extends ../../tsconfig.json, include: ["./**/*"]
├── tsconfig.build.json  # extends ../../tsconfig.build.regular.json, outDir: dist, rootDir: src
├── helpers/
│   └── build.ts         # imports { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build' + { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs', calls void build(adapterConfig)
├── src/
│   └── index.ts         # main exports
└── dist/                # built output (CJS + ESM + .d.ts)
```

**package.json pattern**:
```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/generator-rest-client",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs", 
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": { ".": { "require": { "types": "./dist/index.d.ts", "default": "./dist/index.js" }, "import": { "types": "./dist/index.d.mts", "default": "./dist/index.mjs" } } },
  "scripts": { "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts", "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts", "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build", "test": "vitest run" },
  "files": ["dist"],
  "sideEffects": false
}
```

### Key Types from DMMF (packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts)
```typescript
type Model = {
  name: string           // e.g. "User", "Post"
  dbName: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  documentation?: string
}
type Field = {
  kind: 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'
  name: string           // e.g. "id", "email", "posts"
  type: string           // e.g. "Int", "String", "Post" (for relations)
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  relationFromFields?: string[]  // present for relation fields
  relationName?: string
}
```

### Requirements
The new generator should:
1. Be usable in a Prisma schema as:
```prisma
generator restClient {
  provider = "prisma-generator-rest-client"
  output   = "./generated/rest-client"
}
```

2. Implement `generatorHandler` with `onManifest` and `onGenerate`
3. Parse DMMF models and for each model generate:
   - A TypeScript interface with properly typed fields
   - CRUD methods: `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete`
   - Each method typed with the model's fields
4. Output a single `rest-client.ts` file to the configured output directory
5. Use `src/template.ts` for string template helpers

### Design Considerations
- The generator should use `generatorHandler` from `@prisma/generator-helper` since it's an external/custom generator (not in-process like client-generator-js)
- Need a `bin` entry in package.json pointing to the built entry point, OR the entry point should self-execute calling `generatorHandler` when run directly
- Map Prisma scalar types to TypeScript types (Int→number, String→string, Boolean→boolean, DateTime→Date, BigInt→bigint, Float→number, Decimal→string, Json→unknown, Bytes→Buffer)
- Skip relation fields (kind === 'object') in the interface—only include scalar and enum fields
- The REST client should be framework-agnostic, using `fetch` for HTTP calls
- Need to generate a configurable base URL for the REST client

Please produce a detailed, file-by-file implementation plan with the exact code structure for each file. Include what each function does, its signature, and how data flows through the generation pipeline.

content.text:
I cannot write to the plan file since the Write tool is not available in this read-only session. I will present the complete implementation plan directly in my response.

---

## Detailed Implementation Plan: `packages/generator-rest-client/`

### Architecture Overview

This generator is an **external/custom generator** (not in-process like `client-generator-js`). It is invoked by the Prisma CLI as a child process. The CLI spawns the executable specified in the schema's `provider` field, then communicates via JSON-RPC over stdin/stderr using `GeneratorProcess`. Our entry point must call `generatorHandler` from `@prisma/generator-helper`, which sets up the JSON-RPC listener.

The data flow is:
1. Prisma CLI reads the schema, computes DMMF, and spawns our generator binary.
2. CLI sends `getManifest` JSON-RPC call. Our handler responds with metadata.
3. CLI sends `generate` JSON-RPC call with `GeneratorOptions` (containing `dmmf`, `generator.output`, etc.).
4. Our `onGenerate` handler: extracts models from `dmmf.datamodel.models`, extracts enums from `dmmf.datamodel.enums`, maps each to TypeScript source text using template helpers, and writes a single `rest-client.ts` file to the configured output directory.

### File-by-File Plan

---

#### 1. `packages/generator-rest-client/package.json`

```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/generator-rest-client",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "Prisma generator that outputs a typed REST API client",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "bin": {
    "prisma-generator-rest-client": "dist/bin.js"
  },
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
        "default": "./dist/index.js"
      },
      "import": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.mts",
        "default": "./dist/index.mjs"
      }
    }
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma.git",
    "directory": "packages/generator-rest-client"
  },
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/generator-helper": "workspace:*"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@prisma/dmmf": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*",
    "@types/node": "~20.19.24",
    "typescript": "5.4.5"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build",
    "test": "vitest run"
  },
  "files": [
    "dist"
  ],
  "sideEffects": false
}
```

**Key decisions:**
- `bin` field maps `prisma-generator-rest-client` to `dist/bin.js`. This is the executable name that matches the `provider` string in the Prisma schema. When the Prisma CLI encounters `provider = "prisma-generator-rest-client"`, it resolves this via `node_modules/.bin/prisma-generator-rest-client`.
- `@prisma/generator-helper` is a runtime dependency (provides `generatorHandler`).
- `@prisma/dmmf` and `@prisma/generator` are devDependencies (types only, used for type-checking during development but re-exported transitively through `@prisma/generator-helper`).

---

#### 2. `packages/generator-rest-client/tsconfig.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  "include": ["./**/*"]
}
```

Follows the exact convention of every other package (e.g., `packages/adapter-pg/tsconfig.json`).

---

#### 3. `packages/generator-rest-client/tsconfig.build.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "dist",
    "rootDir": "src"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

Follows the convention seen in `packages/client-generator-js/tsconfig.build.json`.

---

#### 4. `packages/generator-rest-client/helpers/build.ts`

```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { createAdapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build([
  // Build the library entry point (CJS + ESM)
  ...createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }]),
  // Build the bin entry point (CJS only, since it's a Node.js executable)
  {
    name: 'bin',
    format: 'cjs' as const,
    bundle: true,
    entryPoints: ['src/bin.ts'],
    outfile: 'dist/bin.js',
    outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
    emitTypes: false,
    banner: { js: '#!/usr/bin/env node' },
  },
])
```

**Key decisions:**
- Uses `createAdapterConfig` for the library entry (`src/index.ts` to `dist/index.{js,mjs}`), producing both CJS and ESM with types.
- Adds a separate build step for `src/bin.ts` to `dist/bin.js` as CJS only, with a shebang banner. This is the executable that Prisma spawns. No ESM or `.d.ts` needed for the bin entry since it is never imported as a library.
- The `bundle: true` ensures all local source is bundled into each output, while workspace dependencies are externalized (handled automatically by `getProjectExternals` in `helpers/compile/build.ts` which reads `package.json` dependencies).

---

#### 5. `packages/generator-rest-client/src/bin.ts`

```typescript
import { generatorHandler } from '@prisma/generator-helper'

import { onGenerate } from './onGenerate'
import { onManifest } from './onManifest'

generatorHandler({
  onManifest,
  onGenerate,
})
```

**Purpose:** This is the executable entry point. When Prisma spawns this process, `generatorHandler` sets up a readline interface on `process.stdin` and listens for JSON-RPC messages. It dispatches `getManifest` to `onManifest` and `generate` to `onGenerate`, writing JSON-RPC responses to `process.stderr`.

**Design rationale:** Keeping this file minimal follows the pattern in `packages/generator-helper/src/__tests__/minimal-executable`. All logic is delegated to `onManifest` and `onGenerate` modules so the code is testable independently of the JSON-RPC transport.

---

#### 6. `packages/generator-rest-client/src/index.ts`

```typescript
export { onGenerate } from './onGenerate'
export { onManifest } from './onManifest'
export { generateRestClient } from './generateRestClient'
export { mapScalarToTS } from './scalarMapping'
```

**Purpose:** Library entry point. Exports the core generation functions so they can be used programmatically (e.g., in tests or by other tools) without invoking the JSON-RPC handler.

---

#### 7. `packages/generator-rest-client/src/onManifest.ts`

```typescript
import type { GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest } from '@prisma/generator-helper'

export function onManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): GeneratorManifest {
  return {
    prettyName: 'Prisma REST Client',
    defaultOutput: './generated/rest-client',
  }
}
```

**Signature:** `(config: GeneratorConfig) => GeneratorManifest`

**Purpose:** Returns generator metadata to the Prisma CLI. The `defaultOutput` is used when the user does not specify `output` in their schema. The `prettyName` is displayed in CLI output during generation.

**Note:** No `requiresEngines` needed since this generator only reads DMMF and does not need a query engine.

---

#### 8. `packages/generator-rest-client/src/onGenerate.ts`

```typescript
import fs from 'node:fs'
import path from 'node:path'

import type { GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator-helper'

import { generateRestClient } from './generateRestClient'

export async function onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
  const outputDir = options.generator.output?.value
  if (!outputDir) {
    throw new Error('No output directory specified for generator-rest-client')
  }

  const { models, enums } = options.dmmf.datamodel
  const code = generateRestClient(models, enums)

  // Ensure the output directory exists
  fs.mkdirSync(outputDir, { recursive: true })

  // Write the generated file
  const outputPath = path.join(outputDir, 'rest-client.ts')
  fs.writeFileSync(outputPath, code, 'utf-8')
}
```

**Signature:** `(options: GeneratorOptions) => Promise<void>`

**Data flow:**
1. Reads `options.generator.output.value` to determine where to write.
2. Extracts `models` and `enums` from `options.dmmf.datamodel`.
3. Calls `generateRestClient(models, enums)` to produce the full TypeScript source string.
4. Creates the output directory if it does not exist.
5. Writes `rest-client.ts` to the output directory.

**Error handling:** Throws if `output` is null (which would only happen if the manifest `defaultOutput` was not set and the user did not specify output, but Prisma CLI should always resolve this).

---

#### 9. `packages/generator-rest-client/src/scalarMapping.ts`

```typescript
/**
 * Maps Prisma scalar type names to TypeScript type strings.
 */
const SCALAR_TYPE_MAP: Record<string, string> = {
  String: 'string',
  Boolean: 'boolean',
  Int: 'number',
  Float: 'number',
  DateTime: 'Date',
  BigInt: 'bigint',
  Decimal: 'string',
  Json: 'unknown',
  Bytes: 'Buffer',
}

/**
 * Returns the TypeScript type string for a given Prisma scalar type.
 * Falls back to 'unknown' for unrecognized types.
 */
export function mapScalarToTS(prismaType: string): string {
  return SCALAR_TYPE_MAP[prismaType] ?? 'unknown'
}
```

**Signature:** `(prismaType: string) => string`

**Purpose:** Central mapping from Prisma's DMMF scalar type names to TypeScript type literals. This is used by the code generators when emitting interface fields.

**Design rationale:** A simple record lookup with a fallback. Keeping this separate makes it testable in isolation and easy to extend for new Prisma scalar types.

---

#### 10. `packages/generator-rest-client/src/template.ts`

```typescript
/**
 * String template helpers for code generation.
 */

/** Lowercases the first character of a string. */
export function lowerFirst(s: string): string {
  return s.charAt(0).toLowerCase() + s.slice(1)
}

/** Uppercases the first character of a string. */
export function upperFirst(s: string): string {
  return s.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + s.slice(1)
}

/**
 * Creates a plural form for REST endpoint paths.
 * Simple heuristic: appends 's' unless the word already ends in 's'.
 */
export function pluralize(s: string): string {
  if (s.endsWith('s')) return s
  if (s.endsWith('y') && !s.endsWith('ay') && !s.endsWith('ey') && !s.endsWith('oy') && !s.endsWith('uy')) {
    return s.slice(0, -1) + 'ies'
  }
  return s + 's'
}

/**
 * Joins an array of code lines with newlines, filtering out empty strings.
 */
export function lines(...parts: string[]): string {
  return parts.filter(Boolean).join('\n')
}

/**
 * Indents every line of a block by the given number of spaces.
 */
export function indent(text: string, spaces: number = 2): string {
  const pad = ' '.repeat(spaces)
  return text
    .split('\n')
    .map((line) => (line.trim() === '' ? '' : pad + line))
    .join('\n')
}
```

**Purpose:** Pure string manipulation helpers. No DMMF awareness. Used by `generateRestClient` to format generated TypeScript code.

---

#### 11. `packages/generator-rest-client/src/generateRestClient.ts`

This is the core module. It receives the parsed DMMF models and enums and returns a complete TypeScript source string.

```typescript
import type { DatamodelEnum, Field, Model } from '@prisma/dmmf'

import { mapScalarToTS } from './scalarMapping'
import { indent, lines, lowerFirst, pluralize } from './template'
```

**Exported function signature:**
```typescript
export function generateRestClient(
  models: readonly Model[],
  enums: readonly DatamodelEnum[]
): string
```

**Internal structure and data flow:**

The function composes the output by calling several internal helpers in sequence and joining them:

```
generateRestClient(models, enums)
  ├── generateHeader()           → preamble comment + config type + constructor
  ├── for each enum in enums:
  │     generateEnumType(enum)   → TypeScript enum declaration
  ├── for each model in models:
  │     generateModelInterface(model) → TypeScript interface
  ├── generateClientClass(models)     → REST client class with CRUD methods
  └── join all parts into final string
```

**`generateHeader(): string`**

Returns the file preamble:
```typescript
function generateHeader(): string {
  return lines(
    '// This file was generated by prisma-generator-rest-client. Do not edit manually.',
    '// Re-run `prisma generate` to regenerate.',
    '',
    'export interface RestClientConfig {',
    '  baseUrl: string;',
    '  headers?: Record<string, string>;',
    '}',
  )
}
```

**`generateEnumType(enum: DatamodelEnum): string`**

For each enum in `dmmf.datamodel.enums`, generates a TypeScript `enum`:
```typescript
function generateEnumType(e: DatamodelEnum): string {
  const members = e.values.map((v) => `  ${v.name} = "${v.name}",`).join('\n')
  return `export enum ${e.name} {\n${members}\n}`
}
```

Example output for `enum Role { ADMIN USER }`:
```typescript
export enum Role {
  ADMIN = "ADMIN",
  USER = "USER",
}
```

**`generateModelInterface(model: Model): string`**

For each model, generates a TypeScript interface containing only scalar and enum fields (skipping `kind === 'object'` which are relation fields):

```typescript
function generateModelInterface(model: Model): string {
  const fields = model.fields
    .filter((f) => f.kind === 'scalar' || f.kind === 'enum')
    .map((f) => generateFieldLine(f))
    .join('\n')
  return `export interface ${model.name} {\n${fields}\n}`
}
```

**`generateFieldLine(field: Field): string`**

Maps a single DMMF field to a TypeScript interface property line:

```typescript
function generateFieldLine(field: Field): string {
  let tsType: string
  if (field.kind === 'enum') {
    tsType = field.type  // enum name used directly, since we generated it above
  } else {
    tsType = mapScalarToTS(field.type)
  }
  if (field.isList) {
    tsType = `${tsType}[]`
  }
  const optional = !field.isRequired ? '?' : ''
  return `  ${field.name}${optional}: ${tsType};`
}
```

Example: For a model `User` with fields `id Int @id`, `email String`, `name String?`, `posts Post[]`, `role Role`:
```typescript
export interface User {
  id: number;
  email: string;
  name?: string;
  role: Role;
}
```
(Note: `posts` is skipped because `kind === 'object'`.)

**`generateClientClass(models: readonly Model[]): string`**

Generates the main REST client class. The class has:
- A `config` field storing `RestClientConfig`
- A constructor taking `RestClientConfig`
- A private `request` method that wraps `fetch`
- For each model, five CRUD methods

```typescript
function generateClientClass(models: readonly Model[]): string {
  const modelMethods = models.map((m) => generateModelMethods(m)).join('\n\n')

  return lines(
    'export class RestClient {',
    '  private config: RestClientConfig;',
    '',
    '  constructor(config: RestClientConfig) {',
    '    this.config = config;',
    '  }',
    '',
    '  private async request<T>(path: string, options?: RequestInit): Promise<T> {',
    '    const url = `${this.config.baseUrl}${path}`;',
    '    const response = await fetch(url, {',
    '      ...options,',
    '      headers: {',
    '        "Content-Type": "application/json",',
    '        ...this.config.headers,',
    '        ...options?.headers,',
    '      },',
    '    });',
    '    if (!response.ok) {',
    '      throw new Error(`REST client error: ${response.status} ${response.statusText}`);',
    '    }',
    '    return response.json() as Promise<T>;',
    '  }',
    '',
    modelMethods,
    '}',
  )
}
```

**`generateModelMethods(model: Model): string`**

For each model, generates five CRUD methods. Uses `lowerFirst` and `pluralize` from template helpers to derive the REST endpoint path and method names.

```typescript
function generateModelMethods(model: Model): string {
  const name = model.name                    // e.g. "User"
  const endpoint = `/${pluralize(lowerFirst(name))}`  // e.g. "/users"

  // Determine the ID field for findUnique/update/delete
  const idField = model.fields.find((f) => f.isId)
  const idType = idField ? mapScalarToTS(idField.type) : 'string'
  const idParam = idField ? idField.name : 'id'

  // Generate create/update input type (omit fields with defaults for create)
  const createInputName = `${name}CreateInput`
  const updateInputName = `${name}UpdateInput`

  const scalarFields = model.fields.filter((f) => f.kind === 'scalar' || f.kind === 'enum')

  const createFields = scalarFields
    .filter((f) => !f.hasDefaultValue && !f.isUpdatedAt)
    .map((f) => generateFieldLine(f))
    .join('\n')

  const updateFields = scalarFields
    .filter((f) => !f.isId)
    .map((f) => {
      // All update fields are optional
      let tsType = f.kind === 'enum' ? f.type : mapScalarToTS(f.type)
      if (f.isList) tsType = `${tsType}[]`
      return `  ${f.name}?: ${tsType};`
    })
    .join('\n')

  return lines(
    `  // --- ${name} ---`,
    '',
    `  async ${lowerFirst(name)}FindMany(): Promise<${name}[]> {`,
    `    return this.request<${name}[]>("${endpoint}");`,
    '  }',
    '',
    `  async ${lowerFirst(name)}FindUnique(${idParam}: ${idType}): Promise<${name}> {`,
    `    return this.request<${name}>(\`${endpoint}/\${${idParam}}\`);`,
    '  }',
    '',
    `  async ${lowerFirst(name)}Create(data: ${createInputName}): Promise<${name}> {`,
    `    return this.request<${name}>("${endpoint}", {`,
    '      method: "POST",',
    '      body: JSON.stringify(data),',
    '    });',
    '  }',
    '',
    `  async ${lowerFirst(name)}Update(${idParam}: ${idType}, data: ${updateInputName}): Promise<${name}> {`,
    `    return this.request<${name}>(\`${endpoint}/\${${idParam}}\`, {`,
    '      method: "PATCH",',
    '      body: JSON.stringify(data),',
    '    });',
    '  }',
    '',
    `  async ${lowerFirst(name)}Delete(${idParam}: ${idType}): Promise<${name}> {`,
    `    return this.request<${name}>(\`${endpoint}/\${${idParam}}\`, {`,
    '      method: "DELETE",',
    '    });',
    '  }',
  )
}
```

**Top-level `generateRestClient` composition:**

```typescript
export function generateRestClient(
  models: readonly Model[],
  enums: readonly DatamodelEnum[]
): string {
  const parts: string[] = [
    generateHeader(),
    '',
  ]

  // Emit enum types
  for (const e of enums) {
    parts.push(generateEnumType(e))
    parts.push('')
  }

  // Emit model interfaces
  for (const model of models) {
    parts.push(generateModelInterface(model))
    parts.push('')
  }

  // Emit input types for create/update per model
  for (const model of models) {
    parts.push(generateCreateInputType(model))
    parts.push('')
    parts.push(generateUpdateInputType(model))
    parts.push('')
  }

  // Emit the client class
  parts.push(generateClientClass(models))
  parts.push('')

  return parts.join('\n')
}
```

Here `generateCreateInputType` and `generateUpdateInputType` are separated out from `generateModelMethods` to be top-level type declarations rather than inline. This way the input types are reusable:

```typescript
function generateCreateInputType(model: Model): string {
  const scalarFields = model.fields.filter((f) => f.kind === 'scalar' || f.kind === 'enum')
  const fields = scalarFields
    .filter((f) => !f.hasDefaultValue && !f.isUpdatedAt)
    .map((f) => generateFieldLine(f))
    .join('\n')
  return `export interface ${model.name}CreateInput {\n${fields}\n}`
}

function generateUpdateInputType(model: Model): string {
  const scalarFields = model.fields.filter((f) => f.kind === 'scalar' || f.kind === 'enum')
  const fields = scalarFields
    .filter((f) => !f.isId)
    .map((f) => {
      let tsType = f.kind === 'enum' ? f.type : mapScalarToTS(f.type)
      if (f.isList) tsType = `${tsType}[]`
      return `  ${f.name}?: ${tsType};`
    })
    .join('\n')
  return `export interface ${model.name}UpdateInput {\n${fields}\n}`
}
```

### Example Generated Output

For a Prisma schema containing:

```prisma
enum Role {
  ADMIN
  USER
}

model User {
  id    Int     @id @default(autoincrement())
  email String  @unique
  name  String?
  role  Role    @default(USER)
  posts Post[]
}

model Post {
  id        Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  title     String
  content   String?
  published Boolean  @default(false)
  authorId  Int
  author    User     @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id])
}
```

The generated `rest-client.ts` would be:

```typescript
// This file was generated by prisma-generator-rest-client. Do not edit manually.
// Re-run `prisma generate` to regenerate.

export interface RestClientConfig {
  baseUrl: string;
  headers?: Record<string, string>;
}

export enum Role {
  ADMIN = "ADMIN",
  USER = "USER",
}

export interface User {
  id: number;
  email: string;
  name?: string;
  role: Role;
}

export interface Post {
  id: number;
  title: string;
  content?: string;
  published: boolean;
  authorId: number;
}

export interface UserCreateInput {
  email: string;
  name?: string;
}

export interface UserUpdateInput {
  email?: string;
  name?: string;
  role?: Role;
}

export interface PostCreateInput {
  title: string;
  content?: string;
  authorId: number;
}

export interface PostUpdateInput {
  title?: string;
  content?: string;
  published?: boolean;
  authorId?: number;
}

export class RestClient {
  private config: RestClientConfig;

  constructor(config: RestClientConfig) {
    this.config = config;
  }

  private async request<T>(path: string, options?: RequestInit): Promise<T> {
    const url = `${this.config.baseUrl}${path}`;
    const response = await fetch(url, {
      ...options,
      headers: {
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
        ...this.config.headers,
        ...options?.headers,
      },
    });
    if (!response.ok) {
      throw new Error(`REST client error: ${response.status} ${response.statusText}`);
    }
    return response.json() as Promise<T>;
  }

  // --- User ---

  async userFindMany(): Promise<User[]> {
    return this.request<User[]>("/users");
  }

  async userFindUnique(id: number): Promise<User> {
    return this.request<User>(`/users/${id}`);
  }

  async userCreate(data: UserCreateInput): Promise<User> {
    return this.request<User>("/users", {
      method: "POST",
      body: JSON.stringify(data),
    });
  }

  async userUpdate(id: number, data: UserUpdateInput): Promise<User> {
    return this.request<User>(`/users/${id}`, {
      method: "PATCH",
      body: JSON.stringify(data),
    });
  }

  async userDelete(id: number): Promise<User> {
    return this.request<User>(`/users/${id}`, {
      method: "DELETE",
    });
  }

  // --- Post ---

  async postFindMany(): Promise<Post[]> {
    return this.request<Post[]>("/posts");
  }

  async postFindUnique(id: number): Promise<Post> {
    return this.request<Post>(`/posts/${id}`);
  }

  async postCreate(data: PostCreateInput): Promise<Post> {
    return this.request<Post>("/posts", {
      method: "POST",
      body: JSON.stringify(data),
    });
  }

  async postUpdate(id: number, data: PostUpdateInput): Promise<Post> {
    return this.request<Post>(`/posts/${id}`, {
      method: "PATCH",
      body: JSON.stringify(data),
    });
  }

  async postDelete(id: number): Promise<Post> {
    return this.request<Post>(`/posts/${id}`, {
      method: "DELETE",
    });
  }
}
```

### Implementation Sequencing

**Phase 1: Scaffolding (no logic)**
1. Create `package.json` with dependencies and bin entry
2. Create `tsconfig.json` and `tsconfig.build.json`
3. Create `helpers/build.ts`

**Phase 2: Core generation logic**
4. Create `src/scalarMapping.ts` (no dependencies, testable immediately)
5. Create `src/template.ts` (no dependencies, testable immediately)
6. Create `src/generateRestClient.ts` (depends on scalarMapping + template)
7. Create `src/onManifest.ts` (standalone)
8. Create `src/onGenerate.ts` (depends on generateRestClient)

**Phase 3: Entry points**
9. Create `src/bin.ts` (depends on onManifest + onGenerate)
10. Create `src/index.ts` (re-exports)

**Phase 4: Tests**
11. Create `src/__tests__/scalarMapping.test.ts`
12. Create `src/__tests__/template.test.ts`
13. Create `src/__tests__/generateRestClient.test.ts`
14. Create `src/__tests__/onGenerate.test.ts`

**Phase 5: Build and verify**
15. Run `pnpm install` to link the new workspace package
16. Run `pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client build`
17. Verify `dist/bin.js` starts with `#!/usr/bin/env node`
18. Run `pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client test`

---

### Potential Challenges and Mitigations

1. **Shebang in bundled output**: The esbuild `banner` option with `{ js: '#!/usr/bin/env node' }` is the correct approach. After build, verify the file starts with the shebang and is executable (may need a post-build `chmod +x dist/bin.js` step, though npm/pnpm should handle this when the `bin` field is declared).

2. **ReadonlyDeep types from DMMF**: The DMMF types use `ReadonlyDeep<>`, meaning all properties are `readonly`. The generation code only reads from these objects, never mutates them, so this is not an issue. But when constructing test fixtures, you may need `as unknown as DMMF.Model[]` casts or satisfy the full readonly shape.

3. **Composite primary keys**: Some models use `@@id([fieldA, fieldB])` instead of `@id` on a single field. In this case, `model.primaryKey` is set and no individual field has `isId: true`. The plan handles this with a fallback: if no `isId` field is found, fall back to `'id'` as the param name and `'string'` as the type. A future enhancement could support composite keys with a separate `findUnique` signature.

4. **The `pluralize` function is naive**: For production quality, one could use the `pluralize` npm package (already used in `client-generator-ts`). The plan keeps it simple with a local helper for now, but this is an obvious improvement point.

5. **Generator not found at runtime**: When the user writes `provider = "prisma-generator-rest-client"`, the Prisma CLI resolves this name via `node_modules/.bin/`. The package must be installed in the user's project. The `bin` field in `package.json` ensures pnpm/npm creates the symlink.

---

52	  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
53	  plugins: [
54	    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
55	    resolvePathsPlugin,
56	    fixImportsPlugin,
57	    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),
58	    onErrorPlugin,
59	  ],
60	  external: [...(options.external ?? []), ...getProjectExternals(options)],
61	})
62	
63	/**
64	 * Create two deferred builds for esm and cjs. The one follows the other:
65	 * - 1. The code gets compiled to an optimized tree-shaken esm output
66	 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output
67	 * @param options the original build options
68	 * @returns if options = [a, b], we get [a-esm, a-cjs, b-esm, b-cjs]
69	 */
70	function createBuildOptions(options: BuildOptions[]) {
71	  return flatten(
72	    map(options, (options) => [
73	      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
74	      () => applyDefaults(options),
75	      // ... here can go more steps
76	    ]),
77	  )
78	}
79	
80	/**
81	 * We only want to trigger the glob search once we are ready, and that is when
82	 * the previous build has finished. We get the build options from the deferred.
83	 */
84	function computeOptions(options: () => BuildOptions) {
85	  return options()
86	}
87	
88	/**
89	 * Extensions are not automatically by esbuild set for `options.outfile`. We
90	 * look at the set `options.outExtension` and we add that to `options.outfile`.
91	 */
92	function addExtensionFormat(options: BuildOptions) {
93	  if (options.outfile && options.outExtension) {
94	    const ext = options.outExtension['.js']
95	
96	    options.outfile = `${options.outfile}${ext}`
97	  }
98	
99	  return options
100	}
101	
102	/**
103	 * If we don't have `options.outfile`, we default `options.outdir`
104	 */
105	function addDefaultOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
106	  if (options.outfile === undefined) {
107	    options.outdir = getOutDir(options)
108	  }
109	
110	  return options
111	}
112	
113	/**
114	 * Execute esbuild with all the configurations we pass
115	 */
116	async function executeEsBuild(options: BuildOptions) {
117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {
118	    const context = await esbuild.context(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
119	
120	    watch(context, options)
121	  }
122	
123	  const build = await esbuild.build(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
124	  const outdir = options.outdir ?? (options.outfile ? path.dirname(options.outfile) : undefined)
125	
126	  if (build.metafile && options.emitMetafile) {
127	    const metafilePath = `${outdir}/${options.name}.meta.json`
128	    writeFileSync(metafilePath, JSON.stringify(build.metafile))
129	  }
130	
131	  return [options, build] as const
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Execution pipeline that applies a set of actions
136	 * @param options
137	 */
138	export async function build(options: BuildOptions[]) {
139	  return transduce.async(
140	    createBuildOptions(options),
141	    pipe.async(computeOptions, logStartBuild, addExtensionFormat, addDefaultOutDir, executeEsBuild),
142	  )
143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Prints a message every time a new bundle is built
147	 */
148	function logStartBuild(options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions {
149	  console.log(`Building ${options.name} as ${options.format ?? 'cjs'}...`)
150	  return options
151	}
152	
153	/**
154	 * Executes the build and rebuilds what is necessary
155	 * @param builds
156	 */
157	const watch = (context: BuildContext, options: BuildOptions) => {
158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {
162	    ignoreInitial: true,
163	    ignored: [/$src\/__tests__\//, 'package.json'],
164	  } satisfies ChokidarOptions
165	
166	  // prepare the incremental builds watcher
167	  const changeWatcher = createWatcher(['./src'], config)
168	
169	  // triggers quick rebuild on file change
170	  const fastRebuild = debounce(async () => {
171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}
194	
195	// Utils ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
196	
197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)
198	function getOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
199	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
200	    return path.dirname(options.outfile)
201	  }
202	
203	  return options.outdir ?? 'dist'
204	}
205	
206	// get the output file from an original path
207	function getOutFile(options: BuildOptions) {
208	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
209	    const dirname = getOutDir(options)
210	    const filename = path.basename(options.outfile)
211	
212	    return `${dirname}/${filename}`
213	  }
214	
215	  return undefined
216	}
217	
218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external
219	// by having convention in the package.json (dev = bundled, non-dev = external)
220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)
222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled
226	  if (!process.env.IGNORE_EXTERNALS && options.bundle === true) {
227	    return [...new Set([...peerDeps, ...regDeps])]
228	  }
229	
230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled
231	  return []
232	}
233

input.content:
# Plan: `@prisma/generator-rest-client` — Typed REST API Client Generator

## Context

We're adding a new Prisma generator package that reads the DMMF from a Prisma schema and outputs a fully-typed TypeScript REST API client (`rest-client.ts`). The generator runs as an external process (spawned by the Prisma CLI), communicating via the JSON-RPC generator protocol from `@prisma/generator-helper`.

The generated client provides typed interfaces for each model, `CreateInput`/`UpdateInput` types, and a `RestClient` class with `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete` methods per model — all using `fetch` for HTTP calls.

---

## Files to Create

### 1. `packages/generator-rest-client/package.json`

```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/generator-rest-client",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "Prisma generator that outputs a typed REST API client",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "bin": {
    "prisma-generator-rest-client": "dist/bin.js"
  },
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": { "types": "./dist/index.d.ts", "default": "./dist/index.js" },
      "import": { "types": "./dist/index.d.mts", "default": "./dist/index.mjs" }
    }
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build",
    "test": "vitest run"
  },
  "files": ["dist"],
  "sideEffects": false,
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/generator-helper": "workspace:*"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@prisma/dmmf": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*"
  }
}
```

- **`bin`**: Maps `prisma-generator-rest-client` → `dist/bin.js`. This is resolved by the Prisma CLI when the schema specifies `provider = "prisma-generator-rest-client"`.
- **`dependencies`**: Only `@prisma/generator-helper` (runtime; provides `generatorHandler` + re-exports types from `@prisma/dmmf` and `@prisma/generator`).
- **`devDependencies`**: `@prisma/dmmf` and `@prisma/generator` for direct type imports during development.

### 2. `packages/generator-rest-client/tsconfig.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  "include": ["./**/*"]
}
```

### 3. `packages/generator-rest-client/tsconfig.build.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": { "outDir": "dist", "rootDir": "src" },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

### 4. `packages/generator-rest-client/helpers/build.ts`

```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { createAdapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build([
  ...createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }]),
  {
    name: 'bin',
    format: 'cjs',
    bundle: true,
    entryPoints: ['src/bin.ts'],
    outfile: 'dist/bin',
    outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
    emitTypes: false,
    banner: { js: '#!/usr/bin/env node' },
  },
])
```

- Uses `createAdapterConfig` (from `helpers/compile/configs.ts`) for the library entry → CJS + ESM + types.
- Adds a separate CJS-only build for `bin.ts` with a Node shebang banner.
- esbuild's `banner` option is supported natively (the `BuildOptions` type extends `esbuild.BuildOptions`).

---

## Source Files

### 5. `src/bin.ts` — Executable entry point (spawned by Prisma CLI)

Calls `generatorHandler` from `@prisma/generator-helper`, passing `onManifest` and `onGenerate`. This sets up the JSON-RPC stdin/stderr listener. Minimal — all logic delegated to imported modules.

### 6. `src/index.ts` — Library entry point

Re-exports: `onGenerate`, `onManifest`, `generateRestClient`, `mapScalarToTS`.

### 7. `src/on-manifest.ts` — Manifest handler

Returns `{ prettyName: 'Prisma REST Client', defaultOutput: './generated/rest-client' }`. No engines required.

### 8. `src/on-generate.ts` — Generate handler

1. Reads `options.generator.output.value` for the output directory.
2. Extracts `models` and `enums` from `options.dmmf.datamodel`.
3. Calls `generateRestClient(models, enums)` → TypeScript source string.
4. Creates output dir (`fs.mkdirSync` recursive) and writes `rest-client.ts`.

### 9. `src/scalar-mapping.ts` — Prisma → TypeScript type map

Lookup table: `String→string`, `Int→number`, `Float→number`, `Boolean→boolean`, `DateTime→Date`, `BigInt→bigint`, `Decimal→string`, `Json→unknown`, `Bytes→Buffer`. Falls back to `unknown`.

### 10. `src/template.ts` — String helpers for code generation

Pure utilities: `lowerFirst`, `upperFirst`, `pluralize` (simple heuristic), `lines` (join non-empty), `indent`.

### 11. `src/generate-rest-client.ts` — Core generation logic

**Signature**: `generateRestClient(models: readonly Model[], enums: readonly DatamodelEnum[]): string`

**Composition flow** — joins these sections into a single file:

1. **Header**: Generated-file warning + `RestClientConfig` interface (`baseUrl`, `headers?`).
2. **Enums**: For each DMMF enum → TypeScript `export enum` with string values.
3. **Model interfaces**: For each model → `export interface` with scalar + enum fields only (skip `kind === 'object'` relations). Maps types via `mapScalarToTS`; marks optional fields with `?` when `!isRequired`.
4. **CreateInput types**: Per model, includes scalar/enum fields excluding those with `hasDefaultValue` or `isUpdatedAt`. Respects required/optional.
5. **UpdateInput types**: Per model, all scalar/enum fields except `isId`, all marked optional.
6. **RestClient class**:
   - Constructor taking `RestClientConfig`
   - Private `request<T>(path, options?)` method wrapping `fetch` with JSON headers + error handling
   - Per model, 5 CRUD methods:
     - `{model}FindMany(): Promise<{Model}[]>` — GET `/{models}`
     - `{model}FindUnique(id): Promise<{Model}>` — GET `/{models}/{id}`
     - `{model}Create(data): Promise<{Model}>` — POST `/{models}`
     - `{model}Update(id, data): Promise<{Model}>` — PATCH `/{models}/{id}`
     - `{model}Delete(id): Promise<{Model}>` — DELETE `/{models}/{id}`
   - ID field type derived from the field with `isId: true`; falls back to `id: string` for composite keys.
   - Endpoint paths use `pluralize(lowerFirst(model.name))`.

---

## Test Files

### 12. `src/__tests__/scalar-mapping.test.ts`

Table-driven tests for all 9 Prisma scalar types + unknown fallback.

### 13. `src/__tests__/template.test.ts`

Unit tests for `lowerFirst`, `upperFirst`, `pluralize`, `indent`, `lines`.

### 14. `src/__tests__/generate-rest-client.test.ts`

Integration test with stub DMMF models/enums. Asserts:
- Generated output contains enum declarations, model interfaces, input types, client class
- Relation fields (`kind === 'object'`) are excluded from interfaces
- Fields with defaults are excluded from CreateInput
- All UpdateInput fields are optional
- CRUD method names are correct (`userFindMany`, etc.)

### 15. `src/__tests__/on-generate.test.ts`

Tests `onGenerate` with a temp directory:
- Verifies `rest-client.ts` is written to the output dir
- Verifies it throws when no output is specified

---

## Key Reference Files

| File | Why |
|---|---|
| `packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts` | JSON-RPC handler — our `bin.ts` calls this |
| `packages/generator/src/configuration.ts` | `GeneratorOptions`, `GeneratorManifest`, `GeneratorConfig` types |
| `packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts` | `Model`, `Field`, `DatamodelEnum` types we consume |
| `helpers/compile/configs.ts` | `createAdapterConfig` used by our `helpers/build.ts` |
| `helpers/compile/build.ts` | esbuild pipeline (supports `banner` for shebang) |
| `packages/client-generator-js/package.json` | Package.json convention reference |
| `packages/client-generator-js/helpers/build.ts` | Build script convention reference |

---

## Implementation Order

1. **Scaffold**: `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `tsconfig.build.json`, `helpers/build.ts`
2. **Leaf modules** (no internal deps): `src/scalar-mapping.ts`, `src/template.ts`
3. **Core logic**: `src/generate-rest-client.ts` (depends on scalar-mapping + template)
4. **Handlers**: `src/on-manifest.ts`, `src/on-generate.ts` (depends on generate-rest-client)
5. **Entry points**: `src/bin.ts`, `src/index.ts`
6. **Tests**: All 4 test files
7. **Wire up**: `pnpm install` → `pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client build` → `pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client test`

---

## Verification

1. **Build**: `pnpm install && pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client build`
   - Verify `dist/index.js`, `dist/index.mjs`, `dist/index.d.ts`, `dist/bin.js` all exist
   - Verify `dist/bin.js` starts with `#!/usr/bin/env node`
2. **Tests**: `pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client test`
3. **Manual smoke test** (optional): Create a minimal Prisma project with:
   ```prisma
   generator restClient {
     provider = "prisma-generator-rest-client"
     output   = "./generated/rest-client"
   }
   ```
   Run `prisma generate` and inspect the output `rest-client.ts`.

---

## Design Notes

- **Kebab-case file names** per codebase convention (`on-generate.ts` not `onGenerate.ts`).
- **No barrel files** — direct imports between modules.
- **Simple pluralize** — avoids adding the `pluralize` npm dependency for a utility function. Can be upgraded later.
- **Composite primary keys**: Falls back to `id: string` when no single `isId` field exists. A future enhancement could generate tuple-based lookups.
- **No `requiresEngines`** in manifest — this generator only reads DMMF, no engine needed.

3. **Model interfaces**: For each model → `export interface` with scalar + enum fields only (skip `kind === 'object'` relations). Maps types via `mapScalarToTS`; marks optional fields with `?` when `!isRequired`.
4. **CreateInput types**: Per model, includes scalar/enum fields excluding those with `hasDefaultValue` or `isUpdatedAt`. Respects required/optional.
5. **UpdateInput types**: Per model, all scalar/enum fields except `isId`, all marked optional.
6. **RestClient class**:
   - Constructor taking `RestClientConfig`
   - Private `request<T>(path, options?)` method wrapping `fetch` with JSON headers + error handling
   - Per model, 5 CRUD methods:
     - `{model}FindMany(): Promise<{Model}[]>` — GET `/{models}`
     - `{model}FindUnique(id): Promise<{Model}>` — GET `/{models}/{id}`
     - `{model}Create(data): Promise<{Model}>` — POST `/{models}`
     - `{model}Update(id, data): Promise<{Model}>` — PATCH `/{models}/{id}`
     - `{model}Delete(id): Promise<{Model}>` — DELETE `/{models}/{id}`
   - ID field type derived from the field with `isId: true`; falls back to `id: string` for composite keys.
   - Endpoint paths use `pluralize(lowerFirst(model.name))`.

---

## Test Files

### 12. `src/__tests__/scalar-mapping.test.ts`

Table-driven tests for all 9 Prisma scalar types + unknown fallback.

### 13. `src/__tests__/template.test.ts`

Unit tests for `lowerFirst`, `upperFirst`, `pluralize`, `indent`, `lines`.

### 14. `src/__tests__/generate-rest-client.test.ts`

Integration test with stub DMMF models/enums. Asserts:
- Generated output contains enum declarations, model interfaces, input types, client class
- Relation fields (`kind === 'object'`) are excluded from interfaces
- Fields with defaults are excluded from CreateInput
- All UpdateInput fields are optional
- CRUD method names are correct (`userFindMany`, etc.)

### 15. `src/__tests__/on-generate.test.ts`

Tests `onGenerate` with a temp directory:
- Verifies `rest-client.ts` is written to the output dir
- Verifies it throws when no output is specified

---

## Key Reference Files

| File | Why |
|---|---|
| `packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts` | JSON-RPC handler — our `bin.ts` calls this |
| `packages/generator/src/configuration.ts` | `GeneratorOptions`, `GeneratorManifest`, `GeneratorConfig` types |
| `packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts` | `Model`, `Field`, `DatamodelEnum` types we consume |
| `helpers/compile/configs.ts` | `createAdapterConfig` used by our `helpers/build.ts` |
| `helpers/compile/build.ts` | esbuild pipeline (supports `banner` for shebang) |
| `packages/client-generator-js/package.json` | Package.json convention reference |
| `packages/client-generator-js/helpers/build.ts` | Build script convention reference |

---

## Implementation Order

1. **Scaffold**: `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `tsconfig.build.json`, `helpers/build.ts`
2. **Leaf modules** (no internal deps): `src/scalar-mapping.ts`, `src/template.ts`
3. **Core logic**: `src/generate-rest-client.ts` (depends on scalar-mapping + template)
4. **Handlers**: `src/on-manifest.ts`, `src/on-generate.ts` (depends on generate-rest-client)
5. **Entry points**: `src/bin.ts`, `src/index.ts`
6. **Tests**: All 4 test files
7. **Wire up**: `pnpm install` → `pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client build` → `pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client test`

---

## Verification

1. **Build**: `pnpm install && pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client build`
   - Verify `dist/index.js`, `dist/index.mjs`, `dist/index.d.ts`, `dist/bin.js` all exist
   - Verify `dist/bin.js` starts with `#!/usr/bin/env node`
2. **Tests**: `pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client test`
3. **Manual smoke test** (optional): Create a minimal Prisma project with:
   ```prisma
   generator restClient {
     provider = "prisma-generator-rest-client"
     output   = "./generated/rest-client"
   }
   ```
   Run `prisma generate` and inspect the output `rest-client.ts`.

---

## Design Notes

- **Kebab-case file names** per codebase convention (`on-generate.ts` not `onGenerate.ts`).
- **No barrel files** — direct imports between modules.
- **Simple pluralize** — avoids adding the `pluralize` npm dependency for a utility function. Can be upgraded later.
- **Composite primary keys**: Falls back to `id: string` when no single `isId` field exists. A future enhancement could generate tuple-based lookups.
- **No `requiresEngines`** in manifest — this generator only reads DMMF, no engine needed.

# Plan: `@prisma/generator-rest-client` — Typed REST API Client Generator

## Context

We're adding a new Prisma generator package that reads the DMMF from a Prisma schema and outputs a fully-typed TypeScript REST API client (`rest-client.ts`). The generator runs as an external process (spawned by the Prisma CLI), communicating via the JSON-RPC generator protocol from `@prisma/generator-helper`.

The generated client provides typed interfaces for each model, `CreateInput`/`UpdateInput` types, and a `RestClient` class with `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete` methods per model — all using `fetch` for HTTP calls.

---

## Files to Create

### 1. `packages/generator-rest-client/package.json`

```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/generator-rest-client",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "Prisma generator that outputs a typed REST API client",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "bin": {
    "prisma-generator-rest-client": "dist/bin.js"
  },
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": { "types": "./dist/index.d.ts", "default": "./dist/index.js" },
      "import": { "types": "./dist/index.d.mts", "default": "./dist/index.mjs" }
    }
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build",
    "test": "vitest run"
  },
  "files": ["dist"],
  "sideEffects": false,
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/generator-helper": "workspace:*"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@prisma/dmmf": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*"
  }
}
```

- **`bin`**: Maps `prisma-generator-rest-client` → `dist/bin.js`. This is resolved by the Prisma CLI when the schema specifies `provider = "prisma-generator-rest-client"`.
- **`dependencies`**: Only `@prisma/generator-helper` (runtime; provides `generatorHandler` + re-exports types from `@prisma/dmmf` and `@prisma/generator`).
- **`devDependencies`**: `@prisma/dmmf` and `@prisma/generator` for direct type imports during development.

### 2. `packages/generator-rest-client/tsconfig.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  "include": ["./**/*"]
}
```

### 3. `packages/generator-rest-client/tsconfig.build.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": { "outDir": "dist", "rootDir": "src" },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

### 4. `packages/generator-rest-client/helpers/build.ts`

```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { createAdapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build([
  ...createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }]),
  {
    name: 'bin',
    format: 'cjs',
    bundle: true,
    entryPoints: ['src/bin.ts'],
    outfile: 'dist/bin',
    outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
    emitTypes: false,
    banner: { js: '#!/usr/bin/env node' },
  },
])
```

- Uses `createAdapterConfig` (from `helpers/compile/configs.ts`) for the library entry → CJS + ESM + types.
- Adds a separate CJS-only build for `bin.ts` with a Node shebang banner.
- esbuild's `banner` option is supported natively (the `BuildOptions` type extends `esbuild.BuildOptions`).

---

## Source Files

### 5. `src/bin.ts` — Executable entry point (spawned by Prisma CLI)

Calls `generatorHandler` from `@prisma/generator-helper`, passing `onManifest` and `onGenerate`. This sets up the JSON-RPC stdin/stderr listener. Minimal — all logic delegated to imported modules.

### 6. `src/index.ts` — Library entry point

Re-exports: `onGenerate`, `onManifest`, `generateRestClient`, `mapScalarToTS`.

### 7. `src/on-manifest.ts` — Manifest handler

Returns `{ prettyName: 'Prisma REST Client', defaultOutput: './generated/rest-client' }`. No engines required.

### 8. `src/on-generate.ts` — Generate handler

1. Reads `options.generator.output.value` for the output directory.
2. Extracts `models` and `enums` from `options.dmmf.datamodel`.
3. Calls `generateRestClient(models, enums)` → TypeScript source string.
4. Creates output dir (`fs.mkdirSync` recursive) and writes `rest-client.ts`.

### 9. `src/scalar-mapping.ts` — Prisma → TypeScript type map

Lookup table: `String→string`, `Int→number`, `Float→number`, `Boolean→boolean`, `DateTime→Date`, `BigInt→bigint`, `Decimal→string`, `Json→unknown`, `Bytes→Buffer`. Falls back to `unknown`.

### 10. `src/template.ts` — String helpers for code generation

Pure utilities: `lowerFirst`, `upperFirst`, `pluralize` (simple heuristic), `lines` (join non-empty), `indent`.

### 11. `src/generate-rest-client.ts` — Core generation logic

**Signature**: `generateRestClient(models: readonly Model[], enums: readonly DatamodelEnum[]): string`

**Composition flow** — joins these sections into a single file:

1. **Header**: Generated-file warning + `RestClientConfig` interface (`baseUrl`, `headers?`).
2. **Enums**: For each DMMF enum → TypeScript `export enum` with string values.
3. **Model interfaces**: For each model → `export interface` with scalar + enum fields only (skip `kind === 'object'` relations). Maps types via `mapScalarToTS`; marks optional fields with `?` when `!isRequired`.
4. **CreateInput types**: Per model, includes scalar/enum fields excluding those with `hasDefaultValue` or `isUpdatedAt`. Respects required/optional.
5. **UpdateInput types**: Per model, all scalar/enum fields except `isId`, all marked optional.
6. **RestClient class**:
   - Constructor taking `RestClientConfig`
   - Private `request<T>(path, options?)` method wrapping `fetch` with JSON headers + error handling
   - Per model, 5 CRUD methods:
     - `{model}FindMany(): Promise<{Model}[]>` — GET `/{models}`
     - `{model}FindUnique(id): Promise<{Model}>` — GET `/{models}/{id}`
     - `{model}Create(data): Promise<{Model}>` — POST `/{models}`
     - `{model}Update(id, data): Promise<{Model}>` — PATCH `/{models}/{id}`
     - `{model}Delete(id): Promise<{Model}>` — DELETE `/{models}/{id}`
   - ID field type derived from the field with `isId: true`; falls back to `id: string` for composite keys.
   - Endpoint paths use `pluralize(lowerFirst(model.name))`.

---

## Test Files

### 12. `src/__tests__/scalar-mapping.test.ts`

Table-driven tests for all 9 Prisma scalar types + unknown fallback.

### 13. `src/__tests__/template.test.ts`

Unit tests for `lowerFirst`, `upperFirst`, `pluralize`, `indent`, `lines`.

### 14. `src/__tests__/generate-rest-client.test.ts`

Integration test with stub DMMF models/enums. Asserts:
- Generated output contains enum declarations, model interfaces, input types, client class
- Relation fields (`kind === 'object'`) are excluded from interfaces
- Fields with defaults are excluded from CreateInput
- All UpdateInput fields are optional
- CRUD method names are correct (`userFindMany`, etc.)

### 15. `src/__tests__/on-generate.test.ts`

Tests `onGenerate` with a temp directory:
- Verifies `rest-client.ts` is written to the output dir
- Verifies it throws when no output is specified

---

## Key Reference Files

| File | Why |
|---|---|
| `packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts` | JSON-RPC handler — our `bin.ts` calls this |
| `packages/generator/src/configuration.ts` | `GeneratorOptions`, `GeneratorManifest`, `GeneratorConfig` types |
| `packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts` | `Model`, `Field`, `DatamodelEnum` types we consume |
| `helpers/compile/configs.ts` | `createAdapterConfig` used by our `helpers/build.ts` |
| `helpers/compile/build.ts` | esbuild pipeline (supports `banner` for shebang) |
| `packages/client-generator-js/package.json` | Package.json convention reference |
| `packages/client-generator-js/helpers/build.ts` | Build script convention reference |

---

## Implementation Order

1. **Scaffold**: `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `tsconfig.build.json`, `helpers/build.ts`
2. **Leaf modules** (no internal deps): `src/scalar-mapping.ts`, `src/template.ts`
3. **Core logic**: `src/generate-rest-client.ts` (depends on scalar-mapping + template)
4. **Handlers**: `src/on-manifest.ts`, `src/on-generate.ts` (depends on generate-rest-client)
5. **Entry points**: `src/bin.ts`, `src/index.ts`
6. **Tests**: All 4 test files
7. **Wire up**: `pnpm install` → `pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client build` → `pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client test`

---

## Verification

1. **Build**: `pnpm install && pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client build`
   - Verify `dist/index.js`, `dist/index.mjs`, `dist/index.d.ts`, `dist/bin.js` all exist
   - Verify `dist/bin.js` starts with `#!/usr/bin/env node`
2. **Tests**: `pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client test`
3. **Manual smoke test** (optional): Create a minimal Prisma project with:
   ```prisma
   generator restClient {
     provider = "prisma-generator-rest-client"
     output   = "./generated/rest-client"
   }
   ```
   Run `prisma generate` and inspect the output `rest-client.ts`.

---

## Design Notes

- **Kebab-case file names** per codebase convention (`on-generate.ts` not `onGenerate.ts`).
- **No barrel files** — direct imports between modules.
- **Simple pluralize** — avoids adding the `pluralize` npm dependency for a utility function. Can be upgraded later.
- **Composite primary keys**: Falls back to `id: string` when no single `isId` field exists. A future enhancement could generate tuple-based lookups.
- **No `requiresEngines`** in manifest — this generator only reads DMMF, no engine needed.

import type { DatamodelEnum, Field, Model } from '@prisma/dmmf'

import { mapScalarToTS } from './scalar-mapping'
import { lines, lowerFirst, pluralize } from './template'

/**
 * Generates a complete typed REST API client as a TypeScript source string
 * from DMMF models and enums.
 */
export function generateRestClient(models: readonly Model[], enums: readonly DatamodelEnum[]): string {
  const parts: string[] = [generateHeader(), '']

  for (const e of enums) {
    parts.push(generateEnumType(e))
    parts.push('')
  }

  for (const model of models) {
    parts.push(generateModelInterface(model))
    parts.push('')
  }

  for (const model of models) {
    parts.push(generateCreateInputType(model))
    parts.push('')
    parts.push(generateUpdateInputType(model))
    parts.push('')
  }

  parts.push(generateClientClass(models))
  parts.push('')

  return parts.join('\n')
}

function generateHeader(): string {
  return lines(
    '// This file was generated by prisma-generator-rest-client. Do not edit manually.',
    '// Re-run `prisma generate` to regenerate.',
    '',
    'export interface RestClientConfig {',
    '  baseUrl: string;',
    '  headers?: Record<string, string>;',
    '}',
  )
}

function generateEnumType(e: DatamodelEnum): string {
  const members = e.values.map((v) => `  ${v.name} = "${v.name}",`).join('\n')
  return `export enum ${e.name} {\n${members}\n}`
}

function generateModelInterface(model: Model): string {
  const fieldLines = model.fields
    .filter((f) => f.kind === 'scalar' || f.kind === 'enum')
    .map((f) => generateFieldLine(f))
    .join('\n')
  return `export interface ${model.name} {\n${fieldLines}\n}`
}

function generateFieldLine(field: Field): string {
  let tsType: string
  if (field.kind === 'enum') {
    tsType = field.type
  } else {
    tsType = mapScalarToTS(field.type)
  }
  if (field.isList) {
    tsType = `${tsType}[]`
  }
  const optional = !field.isRequired ? '?' : ''
  return `  ${field.name}${optional}: ${tsType};`
}

function generateCreateInputType(model: Model): string {
  const fieldLines = model.fields
    .filter((f) => f.kind === 'scalar' || f.kind === 'enum')
    .filter((f) => !f.hasDefaultValue && !f.isUpdatedAt)
    .map((f) => generateFieldLine(f))
    .join('\n')
  return `export interface ${model.name}CreateInput {\n${fieldLines}\n}`
}

function generateUpdateInputType(model: Model): string {
  const fieldLines = model.fields
    .filter((f) => f.kind === 'scalar' || f.kind === 'enum')
    .filter((f) => !f.isId)
    .map((f) => {
      let tsType = f.kind === 'enum' ? f.type : mapScalarToTS(f.type)
      if (f.isList) {
        tsType = `${tsType}[]`
      }
      return `  ${f.name}?: ${tsType};`
    })
    .join('\n')
  return `export interface ${model.name}UpdateInput {\n${fieldLines}\n}`
}

function generateClientClass(models: readonly Model[]): string {
  const modelMethods = models.map((m) => generateModelMethods(m)).join('\n\n')

  return lines(
    'export class RestClient {',
    '  #config: RestClientConfig;',
    '',
    '  constructor(config: RestClientConfig) {',
    '    this.#config = config;',
    '  }',
    '',
    '  async #request<T>(path: string, options?: RequestInit): Promise<T> {',
    '    const url = `${this.#config.baseUrl}${path}`;',
    '    const response = await fetch(url, {',
    '      ...options,',
    '      headers: {',
    '        "Content-Type": "application/json",',
    '        ...this.#config.headers,',
    '        ...options?.headers,',
    '      },',
    '    });',
    '    if (!response.ok) {',
    '      throw new Error(`REST client error: ${response.status} ${response.statusText}`);',
    '    }',
    '    return response.json() as Promise<T>;',
    '  }',
    '',
    modelMethods,
    '}',
  )
}

function generateModelMethods(model: Model): string {
  const name = model.name
  const endpoint = `/${pluralize(lowerFirst(name))}`
  const createInputName = `${name}CreateInput`
  const updateInputName = `${name}UpdateInput`

  const idField = model.fields.find((f) => f.isId)
  const idType = idField ? mapScalarToTS(idField.type) : 'string'
  const idParam = idField ? idField.name : 'id'

  return lines(
    `  // --- ${name} ---`,
    '',
    `  async ${lowerFirst(name)}FindMany(): Promise<${name}[]> {`,
    `    return this.#request<${name}[]>("${endpoint}");`,
    '  }',
    '',
    `  async ${lowerFirst(name)}FindUnique(${idParam}: ${idType}): Promise<${name}> {`,
    `    return this.#request<${name}>(\`${endpoint}/\${${idParam}}\`);`,
    '  }',
    '',
    `  async ${lowerFirst(name)}Create(data: ${createInputName}): Promise<${name}> {`,
    `    return this.#request<${name}>("${endpoint}", {`,
    '      method: "POST",',
    '      body: JSON.stringify(data),',
    '    });',
    '  }',
    '',
    `  async ${lowerFirst(name)}Update(${idParam}: ${idType}, data: ${updateInputName}): Promise<${name}> {`,
    `    return this.#request<${name}>(\`${endpoint}/\${${idParam}}\`, {`,
    '      method: "PATCH",',
    '      body: JSON.stringify(data),',
    '    });',
    '  }',
    '',
    `  async ${lowerFirst(name)}Delete(${idParam}: ${idType}): Promise<${name}> {`,
    `    return this.#request<${name}>(\`${endpoint}/\${${idParam}}\`, {`,
    '      method: "DELETE",',
    '    });',
    '  }',
  )
}
