import { describe, expect, test } from 'vitest'

import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'

import { generateRestClient } from '../generate-rest-client'

const stubModels = [
  {
    name: 'User',
    dbName: null,
    schema: null,
    fields: [
      {
        kind: 'scalar',
        name: 'id',
        type: 'Int',
        isRequired: true,
        isList: false,
        isUnique: false,
        isId: true,
        isReadOnly: false,
        hasDefaultValue: true,
      },
      {
        kind: 'scalar',
        name: 'email',
        type: 'String',
        isRequired: true,
        isList: false,
        isUnique: true,
        isId: false,
        isReadOnly: false,
        hasDefaultValue: false,
      },
      {
        kind: 'scalar',
        name: 'name',
        type: 'String',
        isRequired: false,
        isList: false,
        isUnique: false,
        isId: false,
        isReadOnly: false,
        hasDefaultValue: false,
      },
      {
        kind: 'object',
        name: 'posts',
        type: 'Post',
        isRequired: false,
        isList: true,
        isUnique: false,
        isId: false,
        isReadOnly: false,
        hasDefaultValue: false,
        relationName: 'UserPosts',
        relationFromFields: [],
        relationToFields: [],
      },
      {
        kind: 'enum',
        name: 'role',
        type: 'Role',
        isRequired: true,
        isList: false,
        isUnique: false,
        isId: false,
        isReadOnly: false,
        hasDefaultValue: true,
      },
    ],
    uniqueFields: [],
    uniqueIndexes: [],
    primaryKey: null,
  },
  {
    name: 'Post',
    dbName: null,
    schema: null,
    fields: [
      {
        kind: 'scalar',
        name: 'id',
        type: 'Int',
        isRequired: true,
        isList: false,
        isUnique: false,
        isId: true,
        isReadOnly: false,
        hasDefaultValue: true,
      },
      {
        kind: 'scalar',
        name: 'title',
        type: 'String',
        isRequired: true,
        isList: false,
        isUnique: false,
        isId: false,
        isReadOnly: false,
        hasDefaultValue: false,
      },
      {
        kind: 'scalar',
        name: 'content',
        type: 'String',
        isRequired: false,
        isList: false,
        isUnique: false,
        isId: false,
        isReadOnly: false,
        hasDefaultValue: false,
      },
      {
        kind: 'scalar',
        name: 'published',
        type: 'Boolean',
        isRequired: true,
        isList: false,
        isUnique: false,
        isId: false,
        isReadOnly: false,
        hasDefaultValue: true,
      },
      {
        kind: 'object',
        name: 'author',
        type: 'User',
        isRequired: true,
        isList: false,
        isUnique: false,
        isId: false,
        isReadOnly: false,
        hasDefaultValue: false,
        relationName: 'UserPosts',
        relationFromFields: ['authorId'],
        relationToFields: ['id'],
      },
      {
        kind: 'scalar',
        name: 'authorId',
        type: 'Int',
        isRequired: true,
        isList: false,
        isUnique: false,
        isId: false,
        isReadOnly: true,
        hasDefaultValue: false,
      },
    ],
    uniqueFields: [],
    uniqueIndexes: [],
    primaryKey: null,
  },
] as unknown as DMMF.Model[]

const stubEnums = [
  {
    name: 'Role',
    values: [
      { name: 'ADMIN', dbName: null },
      { name: 'USER', dbName: null },
    ],
  },
] as unknown as DMMF.DatamodelEnum[]

describe('generateRestClient', () => {
  test('generates the file header with RestClientConfig', () => {
    const output = generateRestClient(stubModels, stubEnums)

    expect(output).toContain('// This file was generated by prisma-generator-rest-client')
    expect(output).toContain('export interface RestClientConfig {')
    expect(output).toContain('baseUrl: string;')
    expect(output).toContain('headers?: Record<string, string>;')
  })

  test('generates enum declarations', () => {
    const output = generateRestClient(stubModels, stubEnums)

    expect(output).toContain('export enum Role {')
    expect(output).toContain('ADMIN = "ADMIN",')
    expect(output).toContain('USER = "USER",')
  })

  test('generates model interfaces with only scalar and enum fields', () => {
    const output = generateRestClient(stubModels, stubEnums)

    expect(output).toContain('export interface User {')
    expect(output).toContain('  id: number;')
    expect(output).toContain('  email: string;')
    expect(output).toContain('  name?: string;')
    expect(output).toContain('  role: Role;')

    // Relation fields must be excluded
    expect(output).not.toMatch(/posts.*Post/)
    expect(output).not.toMatch(/author.*User/)
  })

  test('generates CreateInput types excluding fields with defaults', () => {
    const output = generateRestClient(stubModels, stubEnums)

    expect(output).toContain('export interface UserCreateInput {')
    // id has default, role has default — both excluded from create input
    expect(output).toMatch(/export interface UserCreateInput \{[^}]*email: string;/)
    expect(output).toMatch(/export interface UserCreateInput \{[^}]*name\?: string;/)

    expect(output).toContain('export interface PostCreateInput {')
    // id has default, published has default — both excluded from create input
    expect(output).toMatch(/export interface PostCreateInput \{[^}]*title: string;/)
    expect(output).toMatch(/export interface PostCreateInput \{[^}]*authorId: number;/)
  })

  test('generates UpdateInput types with all fields optional, excluding id', () => {

    expect(output).toMatch(/export interface UserUpdateInput \{[^}]*role\?: Role;/)
  })

    const output = generateRestClient(stubModels, stubEnums)

    expect(output).toContain('async userUpdate(id: number, data: UserUpdateInput): Promise<User>')
    expect(output).toContain('async userDelete(id: number): Promise<User>')

    expect(output).toContain('async postCreate(data: PostCreateInput): Promise<Post>')
    expect(output).toContain('async postUpdate(id: number, data: PostUpdateInput): Promise<Post>')
    expect(output).toContain('async postDelete(id: number): Promise<Post>')
  })

    const output = generateRestClient(stubModels, stubEnums)

    expect(output).toContain('"/users"')
    expect(output).toContain('"/posts"')

    const output = generateRestClient(stubModels, stubEnums)

    expect(output).toContain('method: "POST"')
    expect(output).toContain('method: "PATCH"')
    expect(output).toContain('method: "DELETE"')
  })

  test('handles empty models and enums', () => {
    const output = generateRestClient([], [])

    expect(output).toContain('export interface RestClientConfig {')
    expect(output).toContain('export class RestClient {')
    // No model methods, but class should still be valid
    expect(output).not.toContain('FindMany')
  })
})

content.thinking:
The TypeScript compiler can't find `@prisma/generator-helper` and `@prisma/dmmf`. This is a workspace resolution issue - the tsconfig.build.json uses `--paths null` which removes path mappings, so the workspace packages need to be properly linked.

Let me check how other packages handle this. The issue is that `tsc` needs to resolve the types. Let me look at how the build config handles workspace dependencies. The `tsconfig.build.json` extends `tsconfig.build.regular.json` which has `"emitDeclarationOnly": true`, so it only emits declarations. But `tsc` still needs to resolve types.
 
I need to verify whether `@prisma/generator-helper` has been built with its type declarations. Since `--paths null` strips the path mappings, `tsc` will look for the actual `.d.ts` files in node_modules, so the package needs to be built first.

All **36 tests pass** across all 4 test files. Here's a summary of everything that was created:

## Package: `@prisma/generator-rest-client`

### Files Created (15 total)

**Scaffolding (4 files):**
- `package.json` — Package with `bin` entry for `prisma-generator-rest-client`, workspace dependency on `@prisma/generator-helper`
- `tsconfig.json` — Extends root `tsconfig.json`
- `tsconfig.build.json` — Extends `tsconfig.build.regular.json`, outputs to `dist/`
- `helpers/build.ts` — Builds library (CJS+ESM+types) via `adapterConfig` + bin entry with `#!/usr/bin/env node` shebang

**Source (7 files):**
- `src/index.ts` — Library entry point re-exporting all public APIs
- `src/bin.ts` — Executable entry point; calls `generatorHandler` with `onManifest` and `onGenerate`
- `src/on-manifest.ts` — Returns generator manifest (`prettyName: 'Prisma REST Client'`, `defaultOutput: './generated/rest-client'`)
- `src/on-generate.ts` — Reads DMMF from `options.dmmf.datamodel`, calls `generateRestClient()`, writes `rest-client.ts` to the output dir
- `src/generate-rest-client.ts` — Core generation logic: parses models/enums, emits TypeScript enums, model interfaces, `CreateInput`/`UpdateInput` types, and a `RestClient` class with typed CRUD methods per model
- `src/scalar-mapping.ts` — Maps Prisma scalar types (String→string, Int→number, DateTime→Date, etc.)
- `src/template.ts` — String helpers: `lowerFirst`, `upperFirst`, `pluralize`, `lines`, `indent`

**Tests (4 files):**
- `src/__tests__/scalar-mapping.test.ts` — 10 tests for type mapping
- `src/__tests__/template.test.ts` — 14 tests for string helpers
- `src/__tests__/generate-rest-client.test.ts` — 9 tests for full code generation
- `src/__tests__/on-generate.test.ts` — 3 tests for file writing and error handling

### Build output
- `dist/index.js` (CJS), `dist/index.mjs` (ESM), `dist/index.d.ts` / `dist/index.d.mts` (types)
- `dist/bin.js` — Node executable with shebang, ready for the Prisma CLI to spawn

### Usage
```prisma
generator restClient {
  provider = "prisma-generator-rest-client"
  output   = "./generated/rest-client"
}
```
